summary: Cathy O'Neil announces the publication of her new essay 'On Being a Data Skeptic' by O'Reilly, which builds on Susan Webber's work about the limits of measurement in management. The brief post serves primarily as an announcement with a link to the essay. She adds a humorous aside criticizing the stock photo choice for the cover, suggesting her 11-year-old son could have provided a better skeptical expression.
key_points: New essay 'On Being a Data Skeptic' published by O'Reilly as an e-book
Essay builds on Susan Webber's work arguing that not everything can or should be measured
O'Neil had no input on the cover image selection
money_quotes: I think my 11-year-old son would've done a better job.
key_insight: This announcement post points readers to O'Neil's deeper exploration of why we should question our faith in data and metrics.
